ddls:
CREATE TABLE "volcanic_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each volcanic event */,
  "date_range" TEXT /* Date range of the volcanic event (e.g., 06 Jan-12 Jan 2003) */,
  "deep_volcanic" INTEGER /* Number of deep volcanic (A-type) events */,
  "shallow_volcanic" INTEGER /* Number of shallow volcanic (B-type) events */,
  "emission" INTEGER /* Number of emission events */,
  "tectonic" INTEGER /* Number of tectonic events */,
  "explosion" TEXT /* Notes on explosion events (if any) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the event */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the event */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the event */,
  "magnitude" REAL /* Magnitude or intensity of the event */,
  "depth" REAL /* Depth of the event */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the event */,
  "source_instrument" TEXT /* Instrument or method used to detect the event */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the event (e.g., ongoing, completed, archived) */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event type */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_volcanic_events_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES event_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_volcanic_events_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES event_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the event type (e.g., deep volcanic, shallow volcanic) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event type */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Severity level of the event type */,
  "impact_description" TEXT /* Potential impact of the event type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the event category (e.g., emission, tectonic) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event category */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency or typical occurrence rate of the event category */,
  "associated_hazards" TEXT /* Hazards associated with the event category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event the note belongs to */,
  "note" TEXT /* Additional note for the event */,
  "note_type" TEXT /* Type of note (e.g., observation, analysis, warning) */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the note */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the note was created */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the note */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the note was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_notes_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES volcanic_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the event */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the event was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, download) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_access_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES volcanic_events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
